data.text: The prompt path already has a `PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED` typed error, so one acceptance item is partially present. I’m now tracing where review execution actually chooses a reviewer and where session cost is accumulated, because that will determine whether this bead is a focused server change or a larger cross-cutting slice.

data.text: The review-session package is mostly persistence and prompt assembly. I haven’t found any server-side turn dispatcher yet, which suggests the routing/cost enforcement may need to be wired into the review execution entrypoint rather than `review_session.go` alone. I’m checking the server handler surface next to confirm that before I decide whether this bead is still in-scope.

data.text: The codebase already has cost-cap plumbing in the execute-loop, and the review prompt path already has a typed budget error. That leaves the “server review path” claim in the bead as the critical ambiguity, so I’m checking the bead record itself and nearby tracker context before deciding whether to implement or split.

data.output:
ID:       ddx-f1e12904
Title:    review: cost cap + reviewer routing/downgrade + refusal contracts
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, story:18, area:server, kind:feature
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-42b917fe
Deps:     ddx-0e5c3005
Desc:     PROBLEM
Review sessions have no cost cap enforcement, no reviewer routing policy, and no structured refusal contracts. Without these, a review session can run unbounded cost, use an unavailable reviewer, or fail with an unstructured error that the frontend cannot surface to the operator.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/review_session.go (from ddx-e7b80a50, a dep): ReviewSession has max_billable_usd field but no enforcement code.
- No reviewer routing logic exists in the server review path — preferred_reviewer from Story 17 and the escalation ladder from Story 10 are not consulted.
- No structured refusal error codes exist (PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED, COST_CAP_EXCEEDED, REVIEWER_UNAVAILABLE).
- ddx-0e5c3005 (reviewer routing contract, a dep) must land first.

PROPOSED FIX
- Enforce max_billable_usd per session: before each turn, check cumulative cost_usd against max_billable_usd; if exceeded, return COST_CAP_EXCEEDED refusal.
- Reviewer routing: consult Story 17's preferred_reviewer first; fall back to Story 10's escalation ladder.
- Downgrade routing: when preferred reviewer is unavailable, downgrade to next ladder rung and emit review-pairing-degraded event.
- Implement three structured refusal error codes as typed errors with structured JSON body.

NON-SCOPE
- Turn dispatcher implementation (ddx-f7c3d512, a dep).
- Frontend display of refusal codes (handled by review panel bead).
Accept:   1. max_billable_usd enforced per session: turn rejected with COST_CAP_EXCEEDED when cumulative cost_usd would exceed limit.
2. Reviewer routing consults Story 17 preferred_reviewer first, then Story 10 escalation ladder.
3. Downgrade routing: unavailable preferred reviewer falls back to next ladder rung with review-pairing-degraded event.
4. Three refusal codes implemented: PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED, COST_CAP_EXCEEDED, REVIEWER_UNAVAILABLE.
5. TestReview_CostCap_ReturnsCostCapExceeded verifies enforcement at max_billable_usd.
6. TestReview_ReviewerUnavailable_Downgrades verifies downgrade path and event.
7. TestReview_RefusalCodes_HaveStructuredBody verifies all three codes return JSON body.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

Title:    review: structured prompt assembly + FEAT-022 telemetry + injection delimiters
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, story:18, area:server, kind:feature, triage:no-changes-unverified
Parent:   ddx-42b917fe
Deps:     ddx-e7b80a50
Desc:     Structured prompt assembly per turn (NOT naive verbatim+oldest-first truncation). Pinned: system rubric, artifact identity (path/sha/git_rev), Story 17 template+prompt, first-user-intent + explicit user decisions. Rolling: session memory summary, last K verbatim turns, unresolved findings. FEAT-022 byte accounting. Refuses with PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED if minimum pinned set won't fit. Untrusted-data delimiters (artifact body wrapped) for prompt-injection boundary.
Accept:   1. Assembly function exists with structured pinned + summary + recent + unresolved. 2. FEAT-022 byte accounting per assembly. 3. PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED refusal with clear error. 4. Untrusted-data delimiters wrap artifact body. 5. Tests cover all four.

data.text: I’ve confirmed the blocker: the requested behavior spans the review dispatcher, reviewer selection, cost-cap enforcement, and refusal serialization, but the current server code only has persistence and prompt assembly. There is no server-side turn dispatcher to hook “before each turn” logic into, so this bead can’t be completed cleanly from this worktree without violating the bead’s own non-scope boundary.

data.output:
450		// Service, when non-nil, is the agentlib.FizeauService used to dispatch the
   451		// review invocation. Production callers leave this nil — ReviewBead
   452		// constructs a fresh service from ProjectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir.
   453		Service agentlib.FizeauService
   454		// Runner, when non-nil, replaces the service-based dispatch path. Used by
   455		// tests to return canned *Result values without spinning up a real
   456		// service. Takes precedence over Service.
   457		Runner AgentRunner
   458		// Harness and Model override the reviewer harness/model.
   459		// When empty, Harness defaults to "claude" and Model is resolved
   460		// from TierSmart for the chosen harness.
   461		Harness string
   462		Model   string
   463		// Caps configures the per-section evidence caps used when assembling
   464		// the review prompt (FEAT-022). When zero-valued, evidence.DefaultCaps
   465		// applies.
   466		Caps evidence.Caps
   467		// BeadEvents, when non-nil, is used to append the
   468		// kind:review-pairing-degraded event when the reviewer's resolved
   469		// provider matches the implementer's. Optional: nil disables the
   470		// telemetry emission while preserving the rest of the review flow.
   471		BeadEvents BeadEventAppender
   472	}
   473	
   474	// BuildReviewExecuteRequest constructs the AgentRunRuntime used for the
   475	// post-merge reviewer dispatch. It pins the reviewer harness/model, attaches
   476	// the implementer's correlation map (with role=reviewer overlaid), and
   477	// derives MinPower as impl.ActualPower + 1 so routing is biased toward a
   478	// stronger candidate than the implementer's actual selection. The returned
   479	// runtime is missing per-call plumbing (Prompt/PromptFile, WorkDir,
   480	// SessionLogDirOverride) — the caller fills those in before dispatching.
   481	func BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerModel string) AgentRunRuntime {
   482		correlation := map[string]string{}
   483		for k, v := range impl.Correlation {
   484			correlation[k] = v
   485		}
   486		correlation["role"] = "reviewer"
   487		if impl.Harness != "" {
   488			correlation["impl_harness"] = impl.Harness
   489		}
   490		if impl.Provider != "" {
   491			correlation["impl_provider"] = impl.Provider
   492		}
   493		if impl.Model != "" {
   494			correlation["impl_model"] = impl.Model
   495		}
   496		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
   497			correlation["impl_actual_power"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", impl.ActualPower)
   498		}
   499		minPower := 0
   500		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
   501			minPower = impl.ActualPower + 1
   502		}
   503		return AgentRunRuntime{
   504			HarnessOverride:  reviewerHarness,
   505			ModelOverride:    reviewerModel,
   506			MinPowerOverride: minPower,
   507			Correlation:      correlation,
   508		}
   509	}
   510	
   511	// reviewPairingDegradedBody renders the kind:review-pairing-degraded event
   512	// body. The body carries both implementer and reviewer routing details so
   513	// operators can see exactly which routes converged.
   514	func reviewPairingDegradedBody(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProvider, reviewerModel string, reviewerPower int) string {
   515		var b strings.Builder
   516		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_harness=%s\n", impl.Harness)
   517		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_provider=%s\n", impl.Provider)
   518		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_model=%s\n", impl.Model)
   519		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_actual_power=%d\n", impl.ActualPower)
   520		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_harness=%s\n", reviewerHarness)
   521		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_provider=%s\n", reviewerProvider)
   522		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_model=%s\n", reviewerModel)
   523		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_actual_power=%d\n", reviewerPower)
   524		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "min_power_requested=%d\n", impl.ActualPower+1)
   525		return b.String()
   526	}
   527	
   528	// ReviewBead implements BeadReviewer.
   529	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) ReviewBead(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
   530		b, err := r.BeadStore.Get(beadID)
   531		if err != nil {
   532			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: get bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
   533		}
   534	
   535		// Fetch the git diff for the commit being reviewed.
   536		diff, err := r.gitShow(resultRev)
   537		if err != nil {
   538			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: git show %s: %w", resultRev, err)
   539		}
   540	
   541		// Resolve governing document references.
   542		refs := ResolveGoverningRefs(r.ProjectRoot, b)
   543	
   544		// Determine iteration number from bead events.
   545		iter := 1
   546	
   547		// Build the review prompt under evidence caps.
   548		caps := r.Caps
   549		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
   550			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
   551		}
   552		built := BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, resultRev, diff, r.ProjectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: caps})
   553		prompt := built.Prompt
   554		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
   555		artifacts, err := createArtifactBundle(r.ProjectRoot, r.ProjectRoot, attemptID)
   556		if err != nil {
   557			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: create artifact bundle: %w", err)
   558		}
   559		if err := os.WriteFile(artifacts.PromptAbs, []byte(prompt), 0o644); err != nil {
   560			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: write prompt artifact: %w", err)
   561		}
   562	
   563		// Pre-dispatch short-circuit (FEAT-022 §7): if the assembled prompt
   564		// exceeds MaxPromptBytes after all per-section trimming, skip the
   565		// provider dispatch and return a typed review-error: context_overflow.
   566		// The bead is NOT closed — the loop's review-error event-append path
   567		// records the failure and leaves the bead open for retry.
   568		if built.Overflow {
   569			baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
   570			overflowTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
   571				Sections:    built.Sections,
   572				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
   573				OutputBytes: 0,
   574				Harness:     r.Harness,
   575				Model:       r.Model,
   576			}
   577			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
   578				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
   579				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
   580				Rationale:       evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
   581				ReviewerHarness: r.Harness,
   582				ReviewerModel:   r.Model,
   583				BaseRev:         baseRev,
   584				ResultRev:       resultRev,
   585				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
   586				CostUSD:         0,
   587				InputBytes:      overflowTelemetry.InputBytes,
   588				OutputBytes:     overflowTelemetry.OutputBytes,
   589			}
   590			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
   591				Harness:          r.Harness,
   592				Model:            r.Model,
   593				BaseRev:          baseRev,
   594				ResultRev:        resultRev,
   595				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
   596				BeadID:           beadID,
   597				ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
   598				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
   599			}, reviewArtifactResult{
   600				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
   601				Error:            evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
   602				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
   603			})
   604			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED/context_overflow (assembled prompt %d bytes exceeds cap %d; see %s)",
   605				len(prompt), caps.MaxPromptBytes, artifacts.DirRel)
   606		}
   607	
   608		// Resolve reviewer harness and model. The implementer's harness is
   609		// intentionally NOT used as a fallback — defaulting to it would directly
   610		// violate R4 (different reviewer). When the configured reviewer harness
   611		// is empty we fall through to a fixed default ("claude") regardless of
   612		// what harness ran the implementer.
   613		reviewHarness := r.Harness
   614		if reviewHarness == "" {
   615			reviewHarness = "claude"
   616		}
   617		reviewModel := r.Model
   618		if reviewModel == "" {
   619			reviewModel = ResolveModelTier(reviewHarness, SelectReviewerTier(escalation.TierSmart))
   620		}
   621	
   622		start := time.Now()
   623		runRuntime := BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl, reviewHarness, reviewModel)
   624		runRuntime.Prompt = prompt
   625		runRuntime.WorkDir = r.ProjectRoot
   626		result, runErr := r.dispatchReviewRun(ctx, runRuntime)
   627	
   628		durationMS := int(time.Since(start).Milliseconds())
   629		if runErr != nil {
   630			// Transport-class failure (FEAT-022 §12): network or provider-side
   631			// error. Surface as a typed review-error so the loop classifies and
   632			// counts it correctly, rather than masquerading as a BLOCK verdict.
   633			transportTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
   634				Sections:    built.Sections,
   635				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
   636				OutputBytes: 0,
   637				ElapsedMS:   durationMS,
   638				Harness:     reviewHarness,
   639				Model:       reviewModel,
   640			}
   641			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
   642				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
   643				Rationale:       runErr.Error(),
   644				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport,
   645				ReviewerHarness: reviewHarness,
   646				ReviewerModel:   reviewModel,
   647				BaseRev:         resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev),
   648				ResultRev:       resultRev,
   649				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
   650				DurationMS:      durationMS,
   651				CostUSD:         resultCost(result),
   652				InputBytes:      transportTelemetry.InputBytes,
   653				OutputBytes:     transportTelemetry.OutputBytes,
   654			}
   655			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
   656				Harness:          reviewHarness,
   657				Model:            reviewModel,
   658				BaseRev:          reviewRes.BaseRev,
   659				ResultRev:        resultRev,
   660				Verdict:          string(reviewRes.Verdict),
   661				BeadID:           beadID,
   662				ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
   663				EvidenceAssembly: transportTelemetry,
   664			}, reviewArtifactResult{
   665				Verdict:          string(reviewRes.Verdict),
   666				Rationale:        reviewRes.Rationale,
   667				Error:            reviewRes.Error,
   668				EvidenceAssembly: transportTelemetry,
   669			})
   670			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w", evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport, runErr)
   671		}
   672	
   673		actualHarness := reviewHarness
   674		actualModel := reviewModel
   675		actualProvider := ""
   676		actualPower := 0
   677		if result != nil {
   678			if result.Harness != "" {
   679				actualHarness = result.Harness
   680			}
   681			if result.Model != "" {
   682				actualModel = result.Model
   683			}
   684			actualProvider = result.Provider
   685			actualPower = result.ActualPower
   686			durationMS = result.DurationMS
   687		}
   688	
   689		output := ""
   690		sessionID := ""
   691		if result != nil {
   692			output = result.Output
   693			sessionID = result.AgentSessionID
   694		}
   695		// Strict JSON parse: replaces the legacy markdown extractor that silently
   696		// pulled "BLOCK" from the prompt's options-header line whenever the model
   697		// echoed it back (the upstream-report regression). On parse error we emit
   698		// a typed review-error class — the execute-loop reopens the bead for
   699		// retry rather than mis-recording a BLOCK verdict.
   700		parsed, parseErr := ParseReviewVerdict([]byte(output))
   701		var strictVerdict Verdict
   702		var rationale string
   703		var findings []Finding
   704		if parseErr == nil {
   705			strictVerdict = parsed.Verdict
   706			rationale = strings.TrimSpace(parsed.Summary)
   707			findings = parsed.Findings
   708			if rationale == "" && len(findings) > 0 {
   709				parts := make([]string, 0, len(findings))
   710				for _, f := range findings {
   711					line := strings.TrimSpace(f.Summary)
   712					if line == "" {
   713						continue
   714					}
   715					if f.Location != "" {
   716						line = f.Location + ": " + line
   717					}
   718					parts = append(parts, line)
   719				}
   720				rationale = strings.Join(parts, "\n")
   721			}
   722		}
   723		baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
   724		telemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
   725			Sections:    built.Sections,
   726			InputBytes:  len(prompt),
   727			OutputBytes: len(output),
   728			ElapsedMS:   durationMS,
   729			Harness:     actualHarness,
   730			Model:       actualModel,
   731		}
   732		reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
   733			Verdict:          strictVerdict,
   734			Rationale:        rationale,
   735			RawOutput:        output,
   736			ReviewerHarness:  actualHarness,
   737			ReviewerModel:    actualModel,
   738			ReviewerProvider: actualProvider,
   739			SessionID:        sessionID,
   740			BaseRev:          baseRev,
   741			ResultRev:        resultRev,
   742			ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
   743			DurationMS:       durationMS,
   744			CostUSD:          resultCost(result),
   745			InputBytes:       telemetry.InputBytes,
   746			OutputBytes:      telemetry.OutputBytes,
   747		}
   748	
   749		// R4 pairing-degradation telemetry: when MinPower=actualPower+1 fails
   750		// to push the reviewer onto a different provider than the implementer,
   751		// surface a kind:review-pairing-degraded event so operators can see the
   752		// regression. Best-effort only — emission failures never affect the
   753		// review outcome itself.
   754		if r.BeadEvents != nil && actualProvider != "" && impl.Provider != "" && actualProvider == impl.Provider {
   755			_ = r.BeadEvents.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
   756				Kind:      ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind,
   757				Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("reviewer pinned to same provider as implementer (%s)", impl.Provider),
   758				Body:      reviewPairingDegradedBody(impl, actualHarness, actualProvider, actualModel, actualPower),
   759				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
   760				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
   761			})
   762		}
   763		_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
   764			Harness:          actualHarness,
   765			Model:            actualModel,
   766			SessionID:        sessionID,
   767			BaseRev:          baseRev,
   768			ResultRev:        resultRev,
   769			Verdict:          string(strictVerdict),
   770			BeadID:           beadID,
   771			ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
   772			EvidenceAssembly: telemetry,
   773		}, reviewArtifactResult{
   774			Verdict:          string(strictVerdict),
   775			Rationale:        rationale,
   776			Findings:         findings,
   777			Error:            reviewRes.Error,
   778			EvidenceAssembly: telemetry,
   779		})
   780		if parseErr != nil {

data.text: I’m writing the required no-changes rationale now. It will point to the concrete blocker: the server package does not contain the dispatcher/routing code this bead wants to modify, and the implemented review routing/cost-cap logic already lives elsewhere.

Wrote the required no-changes rationale at [`.ddx/executions/20260505T193318-22288952/no_changes_rationale.txt`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-f1e12904-20260505T193318-22288952/.ddx/executions/20260505T193318-22288952/no_changes_rationale.txt).

Reason: the requested ACs span code that is not present in this server slice. `cli/internal/server/review_session.go` only covers persistence and prompt assembly, while reviewer routing/downgrade logic lives in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go`, and cost-cap enforcement already exists in `cli/internal/server/workers.go`. There is no server-side turn dispatcher here to attach the “before each turn” `max_billable_usd` check to, so completing the bead would require crossing the bead’s non-scope boundary and dependent work.
